# UC-19.1.45 · OpenStack Control Plane RabbitMQ Resource Alarm and Connection Churn

## Description

OpenStack services depend on RabbitMQ for RPC. File descriptor or memory alarms on the broker throttle API and compute operations cluster-wide. Connection churn logs often precede full broker saturation.

## Value

Prevents cascading OpenStack outages by treating RabbitMQ alarms as a Sev-1 signal for the control plane.

## Implementation

Forward RabbitMQ logs only from nodes in the OpenStack control-plane hostgroup (naming convention or CMDB). Tune out known benign restarts. For metrics sourcetype, poll `mem_used`, `fd_used`, `disk_free_limit` and alert on threshold breaches per RabbitMQ clustering guide.

## SPL

```spl
index=openstack (sourcetype="rabbitmq:log" OR sourcetype="rabbitmq:metrics") host=*controller* earliest=-1h
| search "file descriptor" OR "memory alarm" OR "disk alarm" OR "low on disk" OR "connection_closed"
| stats count as alarm_events by host
| where alarm_events>=5
| sort -alarm_events
```

## Visualization

Single value (nodes in alarm), Timeline (alarm keywords), Table (host, count).

## References

- [RabbitMQ — Alarms](https://www.rabbitmq.com/docs/alarms)
- [OpenStack — RabbitMQ for OpenStack](https://docs.openstack.org/ha-guide/shared-database-rabbitmq.html)
